# DocP — Document Parser

Upload a document, ask questions about it in plain English, and get clean, structured
tables back that you can visualize, export, and save. Extraction is powered by
**AWS Textract** (PDFs & images) and **mammoth** (Word docs); answers are generated by
**Anthropic Claude**.

- **Live app:** https://cndocp.vercel.app
- **Repo:** https://github.com/sudarshan-lab/DocP-Document-Parsing

> There are no seeded accounts — open the app and **create an account** to get started.

---

## Features

- **Upload → parse → ask → save** workflow with a GitHub-style, compact UI (dark & light themes).
- **Async extraction:** upload returns immediately; the document is parsed in the background and
  the UI polls until it's `ready`.
- **AI overview** per document: auto summary, key facts, and suggested questions.
- **Chat** with a confirm-before-generate step; each answer can be turned into a table and
  **saved to the database**.
- **Tables ⇄ charts** (bar / line / pie) with **CSV / JSON / copy** export.
- **Organize:** rename documents, add **tags**, search / filter / sort, and a workspace-wide
  **Saved tables** view.
- Documents, Overview dashboard with stats, and Settings pages behind a sidebar layout.

### Supported file types

| Type | Extensions | Extractor |
|------|------------|-----------|
| PDF | `.pdf` | AWS Textract (text + detected tables) |
| Images | `.png`, `.jpg`, `.jpeg`, `.tif`, `.tiff` | AWS Textract |
| Word | `.docx` | mammoth (raw text) |
| Plain text | `.txt`, `.md`, `.csv` | read directly |

> Uploads are capped at **~4.5 MB** because they pass through Vercel's proxy body limit.
> Legacy `.doc` (binary Word) is **not** supported — save as `.docx` or PDF.

---

## Architecture

```
Browser ──▶ Vercel (static React SPA + /api proxy)
                     │  rewrites /api/* ──▶ http://<ec2-host>:9000/api/*
                     ▼
              EC2 (Amazon Linux, Node/Express, pm2 "docp")
                     ├── MongoDB Atlas            (users, files, saved tables)
                     ├── AWS S3                   (original file storage)
                     ├── AWS Textract             (PDF/image extraction)
                     └── Anthropic Claude         (overview + query answers)
```

- The frontend and backend are same-origin **in the browser**: Vercel rewrites `/api/*` to the
  EC2 backend, so there's no CORS or mixed-content (HTTPS page → HTTP API) problem.
- On EC2, AWS access uses the **instance IAM role** (`docp-ec2-role`) by default — no keys on disk.
  Explicit keys are only used if the corresponding env vars are set (useful for local dev).
- Uploaded files are stored **privately** in S3; the viewer uses short-lived **presigned URLs**.

### Tech stack

- **Frontend:** Create React App + TypeScript, react-router-dom, react-dropzone, recharts,
  react-markdown, antd (toasts), dayjs.
- **Backend:** Node.js + Express, Mongoose (MongoDB), `aws-sdk` (S3 + Textract), `mammoth`,
  `@anthropic-ai/sdk`, `multer` (in-memory uploads).

### Repo structure

```
backend/          Express API + extraction pipeline
  app.js          routes, Textract/mammoth extraction, Claude calls
  connection.js   Mongo connection (MONGO_URI)
  File.js, TableResult.js, userModel.js   Mongoose models
  public/         built SPA (only used when the backend serves the UI directly)
client/           React + TypeScript app
  src/pages/      Overview, Documents, Tables, Settings, FilePage, Login, Signup
  src/components/ AppShell, Chatbot, ResultView, TableView, AuthShell, ...
  vercel.json     build config + /api → EC2 rewrite
```

---

## Environment variables (`backend/.env`)

```bash
# Database — a MongoDB Atlas URI in prod; omit to use a local MongoDB for dev
MONGO_URI=mongodb+srv://<user>:<pass>@<cluster>/docp

# Anthropic
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...

# AWS — S3 storage
AWS_BUCKET_NAME=docp-<account>-us-east-2-...
AWS_REGION=us-east-2

# AWS — Textract (usually the same region as the bucket)
TEXTRACT_REGION=us-east-2

# Optional: explicit AWS keys for LOCAL dev only.
# On EC2 these are omitted so the instance IAM role is used instead.
AWS_ACCESS_KEY=
AWS_ACCESS_SECRETKEY=
TEXTRACT_ACCESS_KEY=
TEXTRACT_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=

# Server port (defaults to 9000)
PORT=9000
```

> `connection.js` falls back to `mongodb://127.0.0.1:27017/docp` when `MONGO_URI` is unset.

---

## Running locally

**Prerequisites:** Node.js 18+, a MongoDB (local or an Atlas URI), an Anthropic API key, and AWS
credentials with S3 + Textract access (needed for PDF/image parsing; `.docx`/`.txt` work without AWS).

**1. Backend**

```bash
cd backend
npm install
# create backend/.env with the variables above
npm start           # or: node app.js  → http://localhost:9000
```

**2. Frontend** (in a second terminal)

```bash
cd client
npm install
npm start           # → http://localhost:3000
```

The CRA dev server proxies `/api` to `http://localhost:9000` (see `"proxy"` in
`client/package.json`), so the running backend is reached automatically.

---

## API reference

All routes are under `/api`. Auth is minimal (no JWT); the client stores the returned user in
`localStorage` and passes `userId` where needed.

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `POST` | `/api/signup` | Create an account |
| `POST` | `/api/login` | Log in |
| `POST` | `/api/files` | Upload a file (multipart) → parsed in the background |
| `GET`  | `/api/files?userId=` | List a user's documents |
| `GET`  | `/api/files/:id` | Document details + presigned view URL + saved tables |
| `PATCH`| `/api/files/:id` | Rename and/or update tags |
| `DELETE`| `/api/files/:id` | Delete document (+ its tables + S3 object) |
| `POST` | `/api/files/:id/query` | Ask Claude a question → JSON result (not saved) |
| `POST` | `/api/files/:id/tables` | Save a generated table |
| `GET`  | `/api/files/:id/tables` | List a document's saved tables |
| `DELETE`| `/api/files/:id/tables/:tableId` | Delete a saved table |
| `GET`  | `/api/tables?userId=` | All of a user's saved tables (across documents) |

---

## Deployment

### Backend — EC2 (Amazon Linux) with pm2

The instance runs Node/Express under **pm2** as process **`docp`**, using the **`docp-ec2-role`**
IAM instance role for S3/Textract (no keys on disk). The app lives at
`/home/ec2-user/DocP-Document-Parsing/backend`.

```bash
# copy the changed backend files up
scp -i "<key>.pem" backend/app.js backend/package.json backend/package-lock.json \
    ec2-user@<ec2-host>:/home/ec2-user/DocP-Document-Parsing/backend/

# on the box: install deps, restart, verify
ssh -i "<key>.pem" ec2-user@<ec2-host>
cd /home/ec2-user/DocP-Document-Parsing/backend
npm install --omit=dev
pm2 restart docp --update-env
pm2 logs docp --lines 20
```

> The EC2 checkout is **not** a git repo — deploy by copying files (above), not `git pull`.
> Make sure the EC2 **security group** allows inbound TCP **9000**.

### Frontend — Vercel

The `client/` project is deployed to Vercel. `client/vercel.json` builds the CRA app and rewrites
`/api/*` to the EC2 backend, so the browser only ever talks to the HTTPS Vercel origin:

```json
{
  "rewrites": [
    { "source": "/api/(.*)", "destination": "http://<ec2-host>:9000/api/$1" },
    { "source": "/(.*)", "destination": "/index.html" }
  ]
}
```

Deploy from the `client/` directory:

```bash
cd client
npx vercel --prod
```

> The public domain **cndocp.vercel.app** is the canonical URL. If you point the rewrite at a new
> EC2 host, update the `destination` in `vercel.json`.

### Alternative: single-origin (backend serves the UI)

`npm run build` in `client/` copies the built app into `backend/public/`, and the backend has an SPA
fallback route. So you can also serve everything from the EC2 backend on port 9000 without Vercel.

---

## Security notes

- Passwords are currently stored as-is and auth is unauthenticated beyond a `userId` — this is a
  demo-grade setup, **not** production-hardened. Add hashing (bcrypt) and real sessions/JWT before
  any real use.
- Never commit `backend/.env`. Rotate any credentials that have been shared or exposed.
- Uploaded files are private in S3 and only exposed via short-lived presigned URLs.
